mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary accommodation that lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 consecutive months, it signals a deteriorating labor market that depresses consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, tested over a rolling 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A joint occurrence of rising HOUST (housing starts recovering from cyclical trough) and low/falling FEDFUNDS indicates a housing-led recovery financed by cheap credit, amplifying forward-looking demand expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change > 0 while FEDFUNDS below its 12-month moving average
regime_hint: post-recession housing-led recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: The interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC over single-series HOUST or FEDFUNDS models is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) across walk-forward folds, or joint-trigger instances underperform baseline 60d returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and improving corporate revenue momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in near-term earnings upside.
failure_condition: Correlation between monthly INDPRO change and forward 5-day SPX return is not distinguishable from zero (|corr| < 0.02) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS (retail sales) growth accelerates above its trailing 6-month average by more than 1%, it signals resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue guidance, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds trailing 6-month average growth by > 1pp
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 20d SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.10) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous stabilization in PAYEMS (month-over-month job losses narrowing) and a plateauing UNRATE together signal the labor market inflection point that precedes broad economic recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days that neither series alone reliably captures.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change improves for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE month-over-month change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:labor market typically lags equity market bottoms
regime_hint: labor market inflection near cyclical trough
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to outperform single-series PAYEMS-only or UNRATE-only trigger baselines in forward 60d returns across at least two historical recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime where FEDFUNDS remains near its effective floor for an extended period, further declines in FEDFUNDS lose predictive power for equity returns because rate cuts are exhausted as a policy lever, so the mechanism reverses: forward S&P 500 returns become driven by other signals and FEDFUNDS-only signals produce muted or negative predictive value over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:zero lower bound policy regime post-2008 crisis
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS trend signal continues to produce Rank IC above 0.05 for forward 60d returns even when FEDFUNDS is pinned near the zero lower bound, contradicting the expected loss of signal power.

mechanism_narrative: Following a severe GDPC1 contraction, the first quarter showing a narrowing pace of decline or return to positive quarter-over-quarter growth signals the start of a demand-led recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets re-rate growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change turns positive or less negative after two prior quarters of contraction
evidence_refs: fact:2008-2009 GDP contraction followed by stabilization signals
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns following the GDPC1 inflection signal are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline (p > 0.10) across at least two historical recession-recovery cycles.
